title: abies religiosa subsp. hickelii
intro: Abies religiosa subsp. hickelii, also known as the Hickel's fir, is a subspecies of the Abies religiosa fir tree, native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Abies religiosa subsp. hickelii include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and alleviate pain, making it a popular remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. In traditional herbal medicine, Abies religiosa subsp. hickelii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
